query: My synthetic dataset passes every per-column marginal fidelity test — KS p-values all above 0.5 for continuous columns, total-variation distance under 0.02 for categoricals. But when I train a RandomForest on the synth set and score it on real held-out data, I get ROC-AUC 0.61, while the same model trained on real data scores 0.84 on the same real test set. The marginals look perfect. Why is utility so bad?
expected_behavior: Diagnoses the failure as correlation collapse / joint-structure loss (the SDG preserves per-column marginals but not pairwise or higher-order structure) and recommends computing a pairwise-correlation Frobenius gap between real and synth correlation matrices
Refuses to certify the synth set as usable on the basis of marginal fidelity alone, naming that marginal fidelity is necessary but not sufficient for downstream-task utility
Recommends switching from a marginal-only / independent-column sampler to a joint generator (CTGAN, TVAE, copula-based SDV, or similar) that explicitly models pairwise structure
